content:
Feature selection entered **production ML workflows** as teams moved from one-off statistical modeling to repeatable training pipelines. Early **stepwise regression** scripts were rerun manually; modern **sklearn Pipelines** (2010s) made it practical to bundle selectors with estimators and cross-validation.

**Leakage awareness** grew as Kaggle-style competitions and industry post-mortems showed inflated scores when preprocessing used full datasets. The fix—fit transforms on training folds only—became standard in MLOps checklists alongside [Data Leakage](/topics/data-preprocessing--data-leakage) guidance.

**Feature stores** (Feast, Tecton, internal catalogs) pushed selection outcomes into versioned schemas: which columns are 'model inputs' vs exploratory. Selection shifted from notebook output to **contract** between data engineering and modeling.

**Filter → wrapper** staging emerged for wide data (ads, logs, genomics): cheap univariate filters shrink columns before **RFE** or **Lasso** paths documented in the [dimensionality-reduction feature-selection](/topics/dimensionality-reduction--feature-selection) topic.

Today, embedded **tree importances** and **SelectFromModel** bridge exploration and deployment, while **RFECV** ties subset size to cross-validated metrics rather than guesswork.

timeline.label: Manual stepwise scripts
timeline.description: Analysts reran forward/backward selection outside automated pipelines; reproducibility was weak.

timeline.label: sklearn Pipeline standard
timeline.description: Selectors compose with estimators; CV refits selectors per fold, reducing leakage in benchmarks.

timeline.label: MLOps and feature stores
timeline.description: Selected column sets become versioned contracts between training jobs and serving infrastructure.

timeline.label: Wide-data staging
timeline.description: Filter-then-wrapper patterns scale selection to hundreds or thousands of engineered columns in production.
